description: v1.4.0 perf baseline (P4-T9). Captured against the stress-test-10k fixture (10000 ConfigMaps + 2000 Deployments + 400 Services) across Tier 1 (in-memory) and Tier 2 (PostgreSQL + Apache AGE), on a kind cluster under Docker Desktop / WSL2. Tier 1 is the memory-backed binary run locally against the cluster; Tier 2 is the chart-installed server (CloudNativePG-managed Postgres+AGE) reached over kubectl port-forward, which adds WSL2 networking overhead (~10ms/round-trip vs native Linux). v1.4 adds the F-205 Gatekeeper/Kyverno ENFORCES extractors and the edge-attributes column (Postgres 008 JSONB); these numbers confirm no regression versus the v1.2 10K baseline — cluster_view and namespace_view both land under their 1s targets, and blast_radius stays well under 500ms (the small Tier 2 rise to ~22ms p95 reflects the JSONB attribute scan on the recursive-CTE traversal). spec_targets carry forward the v1.2 reclaimed SLOs unchanged.

spec_targets._comment: Carried forward unchanged from perf-baseline-v1.2.json — the v1.2 reclaimed targets (cluster_view / namespace_view p95 <= 1000ms, blast_radius p95 <= 500ms) remain the live SLOs and v1.4 meets them on both tiers.
